analysis.note: Feminine nominative singular head saṃbhūtiḥ in a complex compound; the sentence is nominal, with an understood 'is/occurs'.

verification.detail: compound stem manovijñānasaṃbhūti as Stri/Prathama/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Temporal adverb modifying the nominal predication: 'always, at all times'.
Segmented as sarvadā + āsaṃjñikāt; the initial ā is absorbed in the continuous printed sandhi sarvadāsaṃjñikād, and final -t appears as -d before ṛte.
Indeclinable governing an exception-list in the ablative.
Underlying ablative singular; final -t surfaces as -n before following m- in middhāt.

verification.detail: compound stem samāpattidvaya as Napumsaka/Panchami/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Underlying ablative singular; final -t surfaces as -n before following m- in mūrcchanāt.
Bhāṣya reading uses cch; final -t voices to -d before api.
Taken additively, 'also/even'; final -i becomes -y before a- of acittakāt.
Privative a- + citta + -ka; taken as an ablative singular adjective agreeing most locally with mūrcchanāt: 'mindless fainting'.

justifications.decision: The feminine nominative singular head is saṃbhūtiḥ, 'birth/origin/production'; the preceding mano-vijñāna is construed as the entity whose arising is being stated, not as a separate subject.
justifications.chosen: manovijñānasaṃbhūtiḥ = 'the arising of mind-consciousness', a genitive tatpuruṣa headed by saṃbhūtiḥ

evidence.translation: MW gives saṃbhūti as a feminine noun meaning birth, origin, or production.

evidence.quote: vi-jñā/na ¦ n. ... understanding, comprehending, recognizing, intelligence, knowledge

justifications.decision: The continuous form is split by ordinary vowel sandhi: sarvadā + āsaṃjñikāt yields sarvadāsaṃjñikāt, and final -t appears as -d before ṛte. sarvadā remains an adverb, while āsaṃjñikāt is the first ablative governed by ṛte.
options_considered: sarvadā as adverb + āsaṃjñikāt as ablative exception
justifications.chosen: sarvadā + āsaṃjñikāt, not a single word sarvadāsaṃjñika

evidence.quote: sarvadā/ ¦ ind. always, at all times
evidence.translation: MW identifies sarvadā as an indeclinable temporal adverb.
MW gives āsaṃjñika as a neuter noun meaning unconsciousness.

justifications.decision: All the following and preceding exception terms are read as ablative singulars dependent on ṛte: āsaṃjñikāt, samāpattidvayāt, middhāt, and mūrcchanāt/acittakāt.
options_considered: ablatives as sources/causes independent of ṛte
ṛte only with āsaṃjñikāt, leaving the later ablatives syntactically separate

evidence.quote: ṛte/ ¦ ind. ... with the exclusion of, excepting, besides, without, unless (with abl. or acc.
evidence.translation: MW states that ṛte means 'excepting/without' and can take the ablative.

justifications.decision: Although printed -ān could be mistaken for accusative plural, the analyzer and syntax with ṛte support ablative singulars; external sandhi before m- accounts for -t becoming nasalized as -n.
options_considered: ablative singulars under ṛte: samāpattidvayāt, middhāt
justifications.chosen: samāpattidvayān middhān are sandhi forms of ablative singular samāpattidvayāt and middhāt

evidence.quote: sam-āpatti ¦ f. ... falling into any state or condition, getting, becoming
evidence.translation: MW supports samāpatti as an attainment or falling into a state.

evidence.quote: dvaya/ ¦ ... twofold, double, of 2 kinds or sorts ... n. id. ... two things, both
evidence.translation: MW supports dvaya as a neuter expression for a pair or twofold set.
MW gives middha as a neuter noun.

justifications.decision: acittakāt is taken as an ablative singular neuter adjective agreeing most locally with mūrcchanāt; api adds this item to the list. Without commentary, broader scope over the whole list remains possible but less immediate grammatically.
options_considered: acittakāt as a separate substantival exception: 'mindlessness'
acittakāt qualifying the whole preceding list
justifications.chosen: mūrcchanād apy acittakāt = 'also from mindless fainting'

evidence.quote: mūrchana ¦ n. (mc.) and f(ā). fainting, swooning, syncope
evidence.translation: MW supports mūrcchana/mūrchana as neuter 'fainting, swooning'.
In the verse phrase, acittakāt has the same ablative singular neuter ending as mūrcchanāt and immediately follows it.

translation:
The arising of mind-consciousness (mano-vijñāna) is at all times—
except for unconsciousness (āsaṃjñika),
the pair of attainments (samāpatti-dvaya), torpor (middha),
and also mindless fainting (mūrcchana).
one_shot_delta: A commentary-blind or sandhi-blind reading could take samāpattidvayān and middhān as accusative plurals in -ān, rather than as ablative singulars with -t > -n before m-.
The fused sarvadāsaṃjñikād could be misread as a single lexical item; grammatically it is best split as sarvadā + āsaṃjñikāt.
The scope of acittakāt is uncertain without commentary; I take it locally with mūrcchanāt, but it might be intended to qualify a broader set of mindless states.
analyzer_disagreements: ByT5 tags āsaṃjñikāt as masculine ablative singular; on the supplied MW entry āsaṃjñika n. 'unconsciousness', I mark it neuter ablative singular. The ablative singular form is identical for masculine and neuter a-stems.
ByT5 gives the lexical spelling mūrchana, while the bhāṣya verse as supplied reads mūrcchana; I follow the supplied bhāṣya spelling for lemma and surface.
ByT5 represents manas and vijñāna as compound premembers and saṃbhūtiḥ as the inflected head; per the requested format I record the whole inflected compound stem manovijñānasaṃbhūti as the lemma.
open_questions: The supplied dictionary gives āsaṃjñika as neuter 'unconsciousness'; the same ablative singular form could also support a masculine technical usage if external evidence required it.
The exact technical rendering of samāpatti as 'attainment' versus 'absorption' cannot be fixed from the supplied MW excerpt alone.
The syntactic scope of acittakāt remains the main unresolved point without commentary.
